examples.scenario: Personalised image — LoRA active (brand face/style consistency)
examples.description: Premium user with a trained LoRA for Acme Skincare. Trigger word auto-injected into prompt.

request.prompt: professional product shot of moisturiser on marble surface, studio lighting

response.prompt_final: acmebrand, professional product shot of moisturiser on marble surface, studio lighting

notes.trigger_word_injected: User did not include 'acmebrand' in their prompt. System injected it automatically. Without this, the LoRA weights activate but have no anchor to apply brand-specific features.
notes.seed_used: Fixed seed 42 makes this image reproducible — user can regenerate identically or use as A/B base.
notes.lora_scale_used: 0.85 = strong brand influence while still following prompt. Scale of 1.0 would override prompt creativity; 0.5 would barely show brand features.

examples.description: User without a trained LoRA, or not passing lora_id. Falls back to base SDXL.

request.prompt: hero banner for a fitness app, bold colours, dynamic athlete

response.prompt_final: hero banner for a fitness app, bold colours, dynamic athlete

examples.description: User uploads 20 reference images. System trains a new LoRA adapter.

notes.async_in_production: In production this endpoint returns 202 Accepted immediately. Training runs as a background GPU job (~15-40 min). User is notified via email/webhook when status becomes 'ready'.
notes.trigger_word: novaderm is now the activation keyword. Include it in any future prompt to activate this LoRA.
